name: Chinese Sweet and Sour Pork
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound pork shoulder, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate pork cubes in soy sauce and salt.
2. In a medium bowl, make batter by mixing flour, cornstarch, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and water until smooth with a fork. Set aside.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok or frying pan.
4. Add onions, carrots, green pepper, and garlic to the hot oil. Stir-fry for 2 minutes over medium-high heat.
5. Add pineapple chunks and stir-fry for 1 minute. Remove the fruit and vegetables from the wok and set aside.
6. Heat oil in a wok or frying pan to 365°F on a thermometer.
7. Add pork cubes to the hot oil, coating them evenly. Cook the pork for 4-5 minutes until crisp and brown, then remove with a slotted spoon. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in an oven set to 200°F.
8. Repeat step 7 with any remaining pork cubes.
9. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and store it in the refrigerator.
10. In a medium saucepan, combine 3/4 cup water, brown sugar, cornstarch, vinegar, catsup, and soy sauce. Heat to boiling, stirring until thickened.
11. Add the vegetables and fruit from step 5 to the saucepan. Cook for 1-2 minutes to heat through.
12. Stir in the pork cubes from steps 7 and 8.
13. Serve immediately with hot cooked rice for 4 servings.
